name: zyte-common-items

metadata.name: zyte-common-items
metadata.summary: Item definitions for Zyte API schema

documentation:
# zyte-common-items

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/zyte-common-items.svg
:alt: PyPI Version
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/zyte-common-items
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/zyte-common-items.svg
:alt: Supported Python Versions
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/zyte-common-items
```

```{image} https://github.com/zytedata/zyte-common-items/workflows/tox/badge.svg
:alt: Build Status
:target: https://github.com/zytedata/zyte-common-items/actions
```

```{image} https://codecov.io/github/zytedata/zyte-common-items/coverage.svg?branch=master
:alt: Coverage report
:target: https://codecov.io/gh/zytedata/zyte-common-items
```

% description starts

`zyte-common-items` is a Python 3.9+ library of [item] and [page object]
classes for web data extraction that we use at [Zyte] to maximize opportunities
for code reuse.

% description ends

- Documentation: <https://zyte-common-items.readthedocs.io/en/latest/>
- License: BSD 3-clause

[item]: https://docs.scrapy.org/en/latest/topics/items.html
[page object]: https://web-poet.readthedocs.io/en/stable/
[zyte]: https://www.zyte.com/

functions.name: zyte_common_items.fields.auto_field
zyte_common_items.fields.is_auto_field
documentation:
Return `True` if the field named *field* of the *cls* page object
class has `auto_field` set to `True` in its field metadata.

All fields defined in {ref}`auto page object classes <auto>` meet this
condition.

classes.name: zyte_common_items.components.breadcrumbs.Breadcrumb
documentation:
A breadcrumb from the [breadcrumb trail] of a webpage.

See {attr}`Product.breadcrumbs
<zyte_common_items.Product.breadcrumbs>`.

[breadcrumb trail]: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Breadcrumb_navigation

classes.name: zyte_common_items.pipelines.AEPipeline
documentation:
Replace standard items with matching items with the old Zyte Automatic
Extraction schema.

This item pipeline is intended to help in the [migration from Zyte
Automatic Extraction to Zyte API automatic extraction](https://docs.zyte.com/zyte-api/migration/zyte/autoextract.html).

In the simplest scenarios, it can be added to the `ITEM_PIPELINES`
setting in migrated code to ensure that the schema of output items matches
the old schema.

In scenarios where page object classes were being used to fix, extend or
customize extraction, it is recommended to migrate page object classes to
the new schemas, or move page object class code to the corresponding spider
callback.

If you have callbacks with custom code based on the old schema, you can
either migrate that code, and ideally move it to a page object class, or
use zyte_common_items.ae.downgrade at the beginning of the callback, e.g.:

```python
from zyte_common_items import ae

...


def parse_product(self, response: DummyResponse, product: Product):
    product = ae.downgrade(product)
    ...
```

classes.name: zyte_common_items.pipelines.DropLowProbabilityItemPipeline
documentation:
This pipeline drops an item if its probability, defined in the settings,
is less than the specified threshold.

By default, 0.1 threshold is used, i.e. items with probabillity \< 0.1 are dropped.

You can customize the thresholds by using the ITEM_PROBABILITY_THRESHOLDS setting that offers
greater flexibility, allowing you to define thresholds for each Item class separately or
set a default threshold for all other item classes.

Thresholds for Item classes can be defined using either the path to the Item class or
directly using the Item classes themselves.

The example of using ITEM_PROBABILITY_THRESHOLDS:

```python
from zyte_common_items import Article

ITEM_PROBABILITY_THRESHOLDS = {
    Article: 0.2,
    "zyte_common_items.Product": 0.3,
    "default": 0.15,
}
```

classes.name: zyte_common_items.components.metadata.Metadata
documentation:
Generic metadata class.

It defines all attributes of metadata classes for specific item types, so
that it can be used during extraction instead of a more specific class, and
later converted to the corresponding, more specific metadata class.

functions.name: zyte_common_items.util.split_in_unknown_and_known_fields
documentation:
Return a pair of dicts. The first one contains those elements not belonging to the
attr class `item_cls`. The second one contains the rest. That is, those
attributes not belonging to `item_cls` class

functions.name: zyte_common_items.util.split_dict
documentation:
Splits the dictionary in two.

The first dict contains the records for which the key predicate is False
and the second dict contains the rest.

```pycon
>>> split_dict({}, lambda k: False)
({}, {})
>>> split_dict(dict(a=1, b=2, c=3), lambda k: k != 'a')
({'a': 1}, {'b': 2, 'c': 3})
```

functions.name: zyte_common_items.util.convert_to_class
documentation:

triggering a run-time warning if any attribute is removed.

classes.name: zyte_common_items.pages.mixins.DescriptionMixin

methods.name: zyte_common_items.pages.mixins.DescriptionMixin.wrap_description_into_html
documentation:
Convert plain text into an article HTML. The format tries to match clear_html.cleaned_node_to_html(). ```pycon >>> DescriptionMixin.wrap_description_into_html('') '
' >>> DescriptionMixin.wrap_description_into_html('foo') '

foo

' >>> DescriptionMixin.wrap_description_into_html('foo bar') '

foo

bar

' >>> DescriptionMixin.wrap_description_into_html('foo bar') '

foo

bar

' >>> DescriptionMixin.wrap_description_into_html(' foo bar ') '

foo

bar

' >>> DescriptionMixin.wrap_description_into_html('foo bar baz ') '

foo

bar

baz

' >>> DescriptionMixin.wrap_description_into_html('2>1') '

2>1

' >>> DescriptionMixin.wrap_description_into_html('

') '

<p>

' >>> DescriptionMixin.wrap_description_into_html('<p>') '

<p>

' ```
Provides description and descriptionHtml field implementations.

documentation: Read an item from a dictionary.

methods.name: zyte_common_items.base.Item._from_dict
documentation: Read an item from a dictionary.

methods.name: zyte_common_items.base.Item.from_list
documentation: Read items from a list.

methods.name: zyte_common_items.base.Item._from_list
documentation: Read items from a list.

methods.name: zyte_common_items.base.Item._apply_field_types_to_sub_fields
documentation:
This applies the correct data container class for some of the fields
that need them.

Specifically, this traverses recursively each field to determine the proper
data container class based on the type annotations. This could handle both
`list` and `object` type requirements. For example:

> - Article having `breadcrumbs: List[Breadcrumb]`
> - Product having `brand: Optional[Brand]`

Moreover, fields that are not defined to be part of data container
classes will be ignored. For example:

> - Article having `headline: Optional[str]`
> - Product having `name: Optional[str]`
Base class for {ref}`items <items>`.

modules.name: zyte_common_items.adapter
documentation: This module offers better integration with the itemadapter package.

functions.name: zyte_common_items.adapter._is_empty
documentation:
Return `True` if the value is to be considered empty for the purpose
of excluding it from serialization.

Empty values include: `None`, empty collections (tuples, lists, etc.).

Non-empty values include: empty `bytes` or `str`, `False`, `0`.

*value* is assumed not to be a mapping, which should be treated as a
non-empty value, but this function would treat as an empty value.

classes.name: zyte_common_items.adapter.ZyteItemAdapter
documentation:
Wrap an {ref}`item <items>` to interact with its content as if it was
a dictionary.

It can be {ref}`configured <configuration>` into [itemadapter] to improve
interaction with {ref}`items <items>` for itemadapter users like [Scrapy].

In extends [AttrsAdapter] with the following features:

- Allows interaction and serialization of fields from
  {attr}`~zyte_common_items.Item._unknown_fields_dict` as if they
  were regular item fields.
- Removes keys with empty values from the output of
  [ItemAdapter.asdict()], for a cleaner output.

[attrsadapter]: https://github.com/scrapy/itemadapter#built-in-adapters
[itemadapter]: https://github.com/scrapy/itemadapter#itemadapter
[itemadapter.asdict()]: https://github.com/scrapy/itemadapter#asdict---dict
[scrapy]: https://scrapy.org/

classes.name: zyte_common_items.adapter.ZyteItemKeepEmptyAdapter
documentation:
Similar to {class}`~.ZyteItemAdapter` but doesn't remove empty values.

It is intended to be used in tests and other use cases where it's important
to differentiate between empty and missing fields.

functions.name: zyte_common_items.processors._format_price
documentation:
Return the price amount as a string, with a minimum of 2 decimal
places.

functions.name: zyte_common_items.processors.only_handle_nodes
documentation:
Decorator for processors that only runs a decorated processor if the
input is of type {class}`Selector` or {class}`HtmlElement`.

functions.name: zyte_common_items.processors.breadcrumbs_processor
documentation:
Convert the data into a list of {class}`~zyte_common_items.Breadcrumb` objects if possible.

Supported inputs are {class}`~parsel.selector.Selector`,
{class}`~parsel.selector.SelectorList`, {class}`~lxml.html.HtmlElement` and
an iterable of {class}`zyte_parsers.Breadcrumb` objects. Other inputs are
returned as is.

functions.name: zyte_common_items.processors.brand_processor
documentation:
Convert the data into a brand name if possible.

If inputs are either {class}`~parsel.selector.Selector`,
{class}`~parsel.selector.SelectorList` or {class}`~lxml.html.HtmlElement`, attempts
to extract brand data from it.

If value is a string, uses it to create a {class}`~zyte_common_items.Brand` instance.

Other inputs are returned unchanged.

functions.name: zyte_common_items.processors.price_processor
documentation:
Convert the data into a price string if possible.

Uses the [price-parser] library.

Supported inputs are {class}`~parsel.selector.Selector`,
{class}`~parsel.selector.SelectorList`, {class}`~lxml.html.HtmlElement` and numeric values.

Other inputs are returned as is.

Puts the parsed Price object into `page._parsed_price`.

[price-parser]: https://github.com/scrapinghub/price-parser

functions.name: zyte_common_items.processors.simple_price_processor
documentation:
Convert the data into a price string if possible.

Uses the [price-parser] library.

Supported inputs are {class}`~parsel.selector.Selector`,
{class}`~parsel.selector.SelectorList`, {class}`~lxml.html.HtmlElement` and numeric values.

Other inputs are returned as is.

[price-parser]: https://github.com/scrapinghub/price-parser

functions.name: zyte_common_items.processors.description_html_processor
documentation:
Convert the data into a cleaned up HTML if possible.

Uses the [clear-html] library.

Supported inputs are {class}`~parsel.selector.Selector`,
{class}`~parsel.selector.SelectorList` and {class}`~lxml.html.HtmlElement`.
Other inputs are returned as is.

Puts the cleaned HtmlElement object into `page._descriptionHtml_node`.

[clear-html]: https://github.com/zytedata/clear-html

functions.name: zyte_common_items.processors.description_processor
documentation:
Convert the data into a cleaned up text if possible.

Uses the [clear-html] library.

Supported inputs are {class}`~parsel.selector.Selector`,
{class}`~parsel.selector.SelectorList` and {class}`~lxml.html.HtmlElement`.
Other inputs are returned as is.

Puts the cleaned HtmlElement object into `page._description_node` and the
cleaned text into `page._description_str`.

[clear-html]: https://github.com/zytedata/clear-html

functions.name: zyte_common_items.processors.gtin_processor
documentation:
Convert the data into a list of {class}`~zyte_common_items.Gtin` objects if possible.

Supported inputs are {class}`str`, {class}`~parsel.selector.Selector`,
{class}`~parsel.selector.SelectorList`, {class}`~lxml.html.HtmlElement`, an
iterable of {class}`str` and an iterable of {class}`zyte_parsers.Gtin`
objects.
Other inputs are returned as is.

functions.name: zyte_common_items.processors.rating_processor
documentation: Convert the data into an {class}`~zyte_common_items.AggregateRating` object if possible. Supported inputs are selector-like objects ({class}`~parsel.selector.Selector`, {class}`~parsel.selector.SelectorList`, or {class}`~lxml.html.HtmlElement`). The input can also be a dictionary with one or more of the {class}`~zyte_common_items.AggregateRating` fields as keys. The values for those keys can be either final values, to be assigned to the corresponding fields, or selector-like objects. If a returning dictionary is missing the `bestRating` field and `ratingValue` is a selector-like object, `bestRating` may be extracted. For example, for the following input HTML: ```html 3.8 out of 5 stars See all 7 reviews ``` You can use: ```python @field def aggregateRating(self): return { "ratingValue": self.css(".rating"), "reviewCount": self.css(".reviews"), } ``` To get: ```python AggregateRating( bestRating=5.0, ratingValue=3.8, reviewCount=7, ) ```

functions.name: zyte_common_items.processors.images_processor
documentation:
Convert the data into a list of {class}`~zyte_common_items.Image`
objects if possible.

If the input is a string, it's used as a url for returning image object.

If input is either an iterable of strings or mappings with "url" key, they are
used to populate image objects.

Other inputs are returned unchanged.

functions.name: zyte_common_items.processors.probability_request_list_processor
documentation:
Convert all objects in *request_list*, which are instances of
{class}`Request` or a subclass, into instances of
{class}`ProbabilityRequest`.

functions.name: zyte_common_items.processors.metadata_processor
documentation:
Processor for a metadata field that ensures that the output metadata
object uses the metadata class declared by *page*.
